titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Fausto Giaccone photographs and papers
titlestmt.author: by Eve C. Neiger

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 12347857.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday June 17, 2021 at 03:04

did.unittitle: Fausto Giaccone photographs and papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In Italian, English, Portuguese, and French.

did.abstract: The collection consists of photographs, papers, and publications that document the work of Italian photojournalist Fausto Giaccone (1943-), from 1966 to 2013. The material includes Giaccone's reportage of social and cultural aspects of Europe, South America, and the Middle East, with an emphasis on political movements in Italy and Portugal from 1966 to 1986. The photographic materials in this collection consist of exhibition prints, work prints, contact sheets, and original slides for fifteen photo journalism assignments; prints for three exhibitions; and slides and work prints for two books, as well as a portfolio of color prints. The papers largely document the production of the book Una storia portoghese (Palermo: Randazzo, 1987), and include clippings and exhibition reviews. The papers also include exhibition catalogs, publications, and publicity material produced for various projects. Some of Giaccone's publications are also included with the collection.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 15 (CDs): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from L'Arengario Studio Bibliografico on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2014.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Fausto Giaccone Photographs and Papers collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
Series IV, 2020 Addition was added in 2021 May.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Fausto Giaccone Photographs and Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Fausto Giaccone (born 1943)
bioghist.p: Fausto Giaccone is an Italian photographer and photojournalist whose work focuses on social movements and cultural issues in Europe, South America, and the Middle East. Born in Tuscany, Italy, in 1943, Giaccone grew up in Palermo and moved to Rome in the mid-1960s to study at the Valle Giulia School of Architecture at the University of Rome. Giaccone began his photojournalism career photographing the student protests at the University in March 1968, an event that became known as the Battle of Valle Giulia. Since the 1960s, Giaccone has worked as a photojournalist, photographing current events for Italian newspapers and other European publications. His subjects have included the Italian student movement of the late 1960s, the Palestinian Fedayeen in Egypt and Jordan, the aftermath of the Carnation Revolution in Portugal, and a survey of European architecture of the 21st century.

His publications include Una storia portoghese (Palermo: Randazzo, 1987); European Millenium Architecture with Roberto Collova (Vienna: Regina Maria Anzenberger, 2003); Volti di Cavallino Treporti (Florence: EDIFIR, 2012); and Macondo: The World of Gabriel Garcia Marquez (Rome: Postcart, 2013).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of photographs, papers, and publications that document the work of Italian photojournalist Fausto Giaccone (1943-), from 1966 to 2013. The material includes Giaccone's reportage of social and cultural aspects of Europe, South America, and the Middle East, with an emphasis on political movements in Italy and Portugal from 1966 to 1986. The photographic materials in this collection consist of exhibition prints, work prints, contact sheets, and original slides for fifteen photo journalism assignments; prints for three exhibitions; and slides and work prints for two books, as well as a portfolio of color prints. The papers largely document the production of the book Una storia portoghese (Palermo: Randazzo, 1987), and include clippings and exhibition reviews. The papers also include exhibition catalogs, publications, and publicity material produced for various projects. Some of Giaccone's publications are also included with the collection.

Photographic projects documented in the collection include reportage of the 1968 student movement in Rome; Palestinian fedayeen in Jordan and Lebanon in 1968 and 1974; the 1981 protest march in Comiso, Sicily; music festivals in Europe; and village life in Southern Italy during the 1970s. Other subjects depicted include fishermen in Mazara del Vallo, Sicily; Paraguan Mennonites; and city and village life throughout Yugoslavia, 1987 to 1990. Highlighted in the collection is Una storia portoghese (A Portuguese Story), a project resulting from two visits Giaccone made to Portugal, in 1975 and 1986, to document rural life in the wake of the Carnation Revolution. Also highlighted in the collection are photographs of Colombia produced for Giaccone's 2013 book , Macondo: The world of Gabriel García Márquez (Rome: Postcard, 2013). Sets of prints are also included from three exhibitions: The Battle of Valle Giulia (American Academy in Rome, Rome, 2008), Viaggio nelle terre dei pastori (Nuoro, 1982), and Sicily 1968-1982 (Salemi, 1982).
Publications included with the collection include Una storia portoghese , the Italian and Portuguese editions; Macondo: The world of Gabriel García Márquez ; and '68 Altrove .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Photographs, 1966-2010. II. Papers, 1975-2013. III. Printed material, 1987-2013. IV. 2020 Addition, 1966-1981.

did.unittitle: Photographs

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized chronologically into 21 groups, according to project. The titles for projects were assigned by Giaccone. Within each group, the photographer separated original prints from later prints. The photographs in each project are arranged chronologically based on the date of image creation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I consists of photographic prints, including silver gelatin prints and inkjet prints; contact prints; and slides printed by Giaccone from 1966 to 2013 for various projects, assignments, exhibitions, and publications.

Additional photographic prints
3 black and white prints
Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II includes textual and digital material compiled by Giaccone for his photographic projects and exhibitions. The largest groups of material document Una storia portoghese and Macondo: The world of Gabriel García Márquez . Documentation for these publications includes typewritten drafts with manuscript corrections and production dummies. Also included is a typewritten transcript of audio recordings made on August 31, 1975, during the Carnation Revolution. The text contains political discussions and dialogues between Portuguese farm workers and Captain Rodrigues of Movimento Farças Armadas. Also included are clippings and reviews for Una storia portoghese as well as exhibition catalogs and publicity material produced for Giaccone's photographic exhibitions.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 16 (computer disks): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Transcription of recording made in Couço, August 31, 1975

L'illustrazione Italiana , Anno VI, Numero 34

Fausto Giaccone original texts written for Una storia portoghese
Original layout for Una storia portoghese
Original invitiation cards for exhibitions of Una storia portoghese
Reviews and clippings
Interview with Antonio Tabucchi published in Expresso
Sandokan , Anno III, Numero 4
Review printed in Journal de Letras, Artes e Ideias , Ano XIX, N. 754
Catalog for the Festival Sete Sóis Sete Luas 1993-1999
Photographic reproduction, inkjet print, of Lotta Continua , September 26, 1975 issue, which first published the photographs taken by Fausto Giaccone in Couço

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In commemoration of the 30th anniversary of the Portuguese Carnation Revolution, Die Tageszeitung published five photographs from Una storia portoghese , printing one photograph a day over the course of a week.

did.unittitle: Diario , Anno V, Numero 3
Noticias Magazine , Numero 726
A Reforma Agrária no Couço: Reencontro com uma História Portuguesa , booklets made by Inês Marques Cardoso, curator of the exhibition held in Couço, April 2006
Texts for Una storia portoghese by Fausto Giaccone

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Promotional material for Fausto Giaccone photograph exhibitions
American Academy in Rome, School of Fine Arts booklet
Exhibition material for 1968: The Battle of Valle Giulia at the American Academy in Rome
Production dummy for Macondo showing the first sequence of photographs
Printed material

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is arranged chronologically by publication date.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III includes copies of publications represented throughout the collection. Also included is '68 Altrove , a catalog of a solo exhibition chronicling Giaccone's early years as a photojournalist.

Series IV. 2020 Addition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of political protests, festivals, and other events, including images made in Rome and other locations in Italy, 1966-1970, 1973-1977, and 1981; London, Crewe, and the Isle of Wight, 1970; and France, 1976. Photograph titles were transcribed from a list provided by Fausto Giaccone. Sequential numbers (FG1-FG381) were transcribed from versos of photographs.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from S. T. de Matteo de Castro, on the William A. Taylor Memorial Fund, 2020.

did.unittitle: Protests against the Vietnam War, 1966-67

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph titles:
Rome, Italy - March 27, 1966. Police in Piazza del Popolo during a demonstration against the Vietnam War. FG1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - March 27, 1966. Demonstration against the Vietnam War in Piazza del Popolo. FG2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - March 27, 1966. Demonstration against the Vietnam War in Piazza del Popolo. Movie actor Gian Maria Volonté among the demonstrators. FG3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - March 27, 1966. Demonstration against the Vietnam War in Piazza del Popolo. FG4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - December 8, 1966. Sit-in against the Vietnam War in Via Veneto, near U.S. Embassy. FG5 - FG6

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - December 8, 1966. Demonstation against the Vietnam War in Via Veneto, near U.S. Embassy. FG7 - FG9

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - December 8, 1966. Demonstation against the Vietnam War in Via Veneto, near U.S. Embassy. Police uses water cannons. FG10 - FG11

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - April 12, 1967. Demonstration against the Vietnam War in Piazza Santi Apostoli. FG12

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - April 12, 1967. Demonstration against the Vietnam War in Piazza Santi Apostoli. Lelio Basso gives his speech. FG13

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - April 12, 1967. Anti-Vietnam War demonstration in Piazza Santi Apostoli. Ferruccio Parri gives his speech. FG14

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - April 15, 1967. Teach-in against the Vietnam War at the Faculty of Physics's lecture hall of La Sapienza University. FG15 - FG16

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - April 15, 1967. Teach-in against the Vietnam War at the Faculty of Physics's lecture hall of La Sapienza University. Film director Marco Bellocchio among the participants. FG17

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portella della Ginestra (Palermo), Sicily, Italy - May 1, 1967. Demonstration on the occasion of the twentieth anniversary of Portella della Ginestra massacre. FG18 - FG22

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 24, 1967. Demonstation against the Vietnam War in Piazza Navona. FG23 - FG24

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 24, 1967. Demonstation against the Vietnam War in Piazza Navona. Enrico Berlinguer, leader of Italian Communist Party (PCI), gives his speech. FG25

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 24, 1967. Demonstation against the Vietnam War in Piazza Navona. FG26

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - July 5, 1966. Demonstration against the Vietnam War in Piazza San Giovanni. Writer and painter Calo Levi on stage. FG27

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - July 5, 1966. Demonstration against the Vietnam War in Piazza San Giovanni. FG28 - FG30

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - November 29, 1967. March against the Vietnam War. Social activist Danilo Dolci parade in Piazza Venezia together with Lorenzo Barbera, Ernesto Treccani and representatives of Northern Vietnam government. FG31

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - November 29, 1967. March against the Vietnam War. FG32 - FG36

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - November 29, 1967. March against the Vietnam War. Some demonstrators hold placards with a portrait of Mikīs Theodōrakīs, a Greek composer arrested and tortured by the military junta. FG37

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - November 29, 1967. March against the Vietnam War. The rally ends in Piazza della Repubblica (aka Piazza Esedra). FG38

did.unittitle: 1968 Student Protests/University of Rome

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph titles:
Rome, Italy - February 27, 1968. Piazza Santi Apostoli, demostration against Student Movement called by "La Caravella", Roman university far-right group. FG40 - FG42

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 1, 1967. Via Merulana, Student Movement joins the May Day demonstration organized by trade unions. FG43

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 1, 1967. Student Movement joins the May Day demonstration organized by trade unions. FG44 - FG46

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - July 11, 1968. La Sapienza University, Main Hall of the Department of Human Studies, assembly in solidarity with arrested students, including Oreste Scalzone and Franco Piperno. Among the participants Jacques Sauvageot and other representatives of French student movement. FG47

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - July 11, 1968. La Sapienza University, Main Hall of the Department of Human Studies, assembly in solidarity with arrested students, including Oreste Scalzone and Franco Piperno. Among the participants Jacques Sauvageot (speaking) and other representatives of French student movement. FG48 - FG49

Battipaglia (Salerno), Italy - 11 aprile 1969. The city after "la rivolta di Battipaglia": two days of popular revolt against closing of two plants. FG74 - FG85

scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - February 6, 1970. Neo-fascists outside La Sapienza University's Law Faculty during a demostration against neo-fascism and the police repression. FG109

scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - February 6, 1970. Neo-fascists outside La Sapienza University's Law Faculty during a demostration against neo-fascism and the police repression. Among them Bruno Di Luia (first from left) and Giancarlo Cartocci (in the second row). FG110

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - February 6, 1970. Neo-fascists outside La Sapienza University's Law Faculty during a demostration against neo-fascism and the police repression. FG111 - FG113

scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - February 6, 1970. Neo-fascists and police outside La Sapienza University's Law Faculty during a demostration against neo-fascism and the police repression. FG114 - FG117

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - February 6, 1970. A Neo-fascist does the nazi salute during a demostration against neo-fascism and the police repression at La Sapienza University. FG118

scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - February 6, 1970. Police at La Sapienza University during a demostration against neo-fascism and the police repression. FG119 - FG120

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - February 15, 1970. First public meeting of "Il manifesto" group at Teatro Eliseo. From left: Lucio Magri, Rossana Rossanda, Massimo Caprara, Filippo Maone, Ninetta Zandigiacomi, Michele Rago, Marcello Cini, Luciana Castellina. FG121

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - February 15, 1970. First public meeting of "Il manifesto" group at Teatro Eliseo. From left: Rossana Rossanda, Luigi Pintor, Lucio Magri. FG122

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - February 15, 1970. First public meeting of "Il manifesto" group at Teatro Eliseo. From right: Aldo Natoli, Rossana Rossanda and three young people. FG123

did.unittitle: International March against Militarism, 1976

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph titles:
Palmanova (Udine), Italy - July 29-30, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). FG240

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Between Palmanova and Udine, Friuli, Italy - July 31, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). FG241 - FG245

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Between Udine and Peschiera del Garda, Italy - July 31, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). FG246

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Peschiera del Garda (Verona), Italy - August 1, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). The demonstrators head for military prison. On the right, with a placard round his neck, Jan Fabre. FG247 - FG248

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Peschiera del Garda (Verona), Italy - August 1, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). The demonstrators in front of the military prison. Among them, with a placard round his neck, Jan Fabre. FG249

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Peschiera del Garda (Verona), Italy - August 1, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). Sit-in in front of the military prison. FG250

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Between Metz and Gravelotte, France - August 5, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). FG251 - FG254

scopecontent.p: Douaumont, France - August 8, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). FG255 - FG260

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Verdun, France - August 9, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). FG261 - FG262

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Orgosolo (Nuoro), Sardinia, Italy - August 15, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). FG263 - FG265

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Palau (Sassari), Sardinia, Italy - August 18, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). On the ferry to Island of La Maddalena. FG266

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: La Maddalena (Sassari), Sardinia, Italy - August 19, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). FG267 - FG270

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: La Maddalena (Sassari), Sardinia, Italy - August 19, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). Among the participants, Julian Beck, founder of "The Living Theatre". FG271

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: La Maddalena (Sassari), Sardinia, Italy - August 19, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). Julian Beck (left), founder of "The Living Theatre", performs an happening together with other march participants. FG272

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: La Maddalena (Sassari), Sardinia, Italy - August 19, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). FG273 - FG274

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: La Maddalena (Sassari), Sardinia, Italy - August 19, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). Marco Pannella giving instrucions to the nonviolent activists the police is about to charge. FG275

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: La Maddalena (Sassari), Sardinia, Italy - August 19, 1976. Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). Police against nonviolent activists. FG276 - FG277

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sardinia, Italy, between 13 and 19 August, 1976. At night during the Antimilitarist march called by Partito Radicale (Radical Party). FG278 - FG280

did.unittitle: Anti-Nuclear Demonstrations 1976-1977

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph titles:
Capalbio Scalo (Grosseto), Italy - January 30, 1977. Demonstration against the installation of the nuclear power plant in Montalto di Castro. FG281

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Capalbio Scalo (Grosseto), Italy - January 30, 1977. Demonstration against the installation of the nuclear power plant in Montalto di Castro. Nicola Caracciolo's speech. Photographer Paola Agosti at work (behind microphone wire). FG282

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Capalbio Scalo (Grosseto), Italy - January 30, 1977. Demonstration against the installation of the nuclear power plant in Montalto di Castro. FG283 - FG287

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Capalbio Scalo (Grosseto), Italy - January 30, 1977. Demonstration against the installation of the nuclear power plant in Montalto di Castro. Carlo Caracciolo among the protesters. FG288

scopecontent.p: Capalbio Scalo (Grosseto), Italy - January 30, 1977. Demonstration against the installation of the nuclear power plant in Montalto di Castro. FG289 - FG290

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Montalto di Castro (Viterbo), Italy - January 1977. A shepherd driving his sheep. FG291 - FG293

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Montalto di Castro (Viterbo), Italy - March 20, 1977. Spring Festival of the Movement on the meadows of Pian dei Gagani, where a new nuclear power plant is supposed to be set up. FG310 - FG327

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Montalto di Castro (Viterbo), Italy - March 20, 1977. Spring Festival of the Movement on the meadows of Pian dei Gagani, where a new nuclear power plant is supposed to be set up. Photographers Romano Martinis and Tano D'Amico. FG328

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Montalto di Castro (Viterbo), Italy - March 20, 1977. Spring Festival of the Movement on the meadows of Pian dei Gagani, where a new nuclear power plant is supposed to be set up. Photographer Tano D'Amico. FG329

scopecontent.p: Montalto di Castro (Viterbo), Italy - July 30, 1977. Demonstration against the installation of the nuclear power plant. FG345

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Movement 77 Protests, Rome

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph titles:
Rome, Italy - March 5, 1977. City centre after a day of street protest and riots. FG299

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - March 5, 1977. Policemen on the city centre streets after a day of protest and riots. FG300

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - March 5, 1977. Car on fire after a day of street protest and riots. FG301

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - March 12, 1977. Police in Via del Corso during a demonstration. FG302

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - March 12, 1977. Policemen on the street. Photographer Tano D'Amico at work (left). FG303

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - March 12, 1977. Policeman pulling over a man. FG304 - FG305

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - March 12, 1977. City centre after a day of street protest and riots. FG306 - FG307

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Italy, Rome, March 12, 1977. Police in the city centre streets after a day of protest and riots. FG308

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Italy, Rome, March 12, 1977. Policemen and photographers in the city centre streeets. FG309

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 12, 1977. The day Giorgiana Masi was shot. Demostration called by Partito Radicale in Piazza Navona on the third anniversary of victory in referedum on divorce. On stage Gianfranco Spadaccia and Angiolo Bandinelli (second and fourth from left). FG330

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 12, 1977. The day Giorgiana Masi was shot. Nonviolent protest called by Partito Radicale before the Senate to challenge the ban on demonstrations until May 31 imposed by Minister of the Interior Francesco Cossiga. FG331 - FG333

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 12, 1977. The day Giorgiana Masi was shot. Radical Party (Partito Radicale) leaders Mauro Mellini and Adele Faccio partecipate in nonviolent protest before the Senate to challenge the ban on demonstrations until May 31 imposed by Minister of the Interior Francesco Cossiga. FG334

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 12, 1977. The day Giorgiana Masi was shot. Clashes between demonstrators and police forces in Corso Vittorio Emanuele II, in front of Sant'Andrea della Valle Church. FG335

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 12, 1977. The day Giorgiana Masi was shot. Clashes between demonstrators and police forces in Corso Vittorio Emanuele II, close to Piazza della Cancelleria. On the right Giovanni Santone, the palinclothes policeman that Tano D'Amico photographed that day while holding a gun. FG336

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 12, 1977. The day Giorgiana Masi was shot. Clashes between demonstrators and police forces. FG337 - FG338

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 12, 1977. The day Giorgiana Masi was shot. Clashes between demonstrators and police forces in Piazza del Teatro di Pompeo. FG339

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 12, 1977. The day Giorgiana Masi was shot. Clashes between demonstrators and police forces near Campo de' Fiori. FG340

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - May 16, 1977. Giorgiana Masi's funeral. FG341 - FG344

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bologna, Italy - October 24, 1977. The Movement in Piazza Maggiore during the International Convention against Repression. FG348

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bologna, Italy - October 24, 1977. The Movement in the streets during the International Convention against Repression. FG349 - FG350

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rome, Italy - October 1, 1977. Clashes on the streets after the death of Walter Rossi, young Lotta Continua activist. FG354 - FG355

did.unittitle: Protests against US Cruise Missiles 1981

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph titles:
Comiso, Sicily, Italy - October 11, 1981. Demonstration against the installation of the Cruise missiles in the ex airport of the town, new NATO base. FG367 - FG376

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comiso, Sicily, Italy - October 11, 1981. Demonstration against the installation of the Cruise missiles in the ex airport of the town, new NATO base. March ends in Piazza Fonte Diana. FG377 - FG378

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comiso, Sicily, Italy - October 11, 1981. Selling "L'Unità", the Italian Communist Party newspapaer, the day of the demonstration against the installation of the Cruise missiles in the ex airport of the town, new NATO base. FG379

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comiso, Sicily, Italy - October 11, 1981. Out of Italian Communist Party' headquarters, the day of the demonstration against the installation of the Cruise missiles in the ex airport of the town, new NATO base. FG380 - FG381
